page: Microsoft Azure GS5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor with an x86_64 architecture. The CPU operates at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz and includes 40 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 448.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 14.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled with a capacity of 918 GB, and there are no integrated GPUs.

Benchmark results indicate a clear performance tradeoff between single-core and multi-core operations. Single-core performance is relatively low, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranking in the bottom 10% and Geekbench single-core scores in the bottom 25%. However, multi-core execution scales to average levels, as demonstrated by average ratings in stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth for both small and large blocks remains in the average tier.

Given the high memory density of 14.0 GB per core alongside average multi-threaded performance, this server is configured for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high single-thread clock speeds. Factual benchmark data shows average performance in database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving. It is suitable for large-scale databases, caching layers, and memory-heavy enterprise applications, though its older CPU architecture and weak single-core metrics represent a tradeoff in raw compute efficiency per core.
description: Microsoft Azure GS5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor running at 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with 448.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 14.0 GB per core, and includes 918 GB of local storage. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the instance exhibits a distinct performance tradeoff: single-core benchmarks, such as stress-ng and Geekbench single-core, rank in the bottom 10% to 25%, whereas multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations consistently score in the average tier. Lacking GPU acceleration, this server is designed for memory-intensive workloads, including large databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications that benefit from high memory capacity rather than high single-threaded compute speeds.
og_description: Microsoft Azure GS5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 448 GB of RAM, and 918 GB of local storage, delivering average multi-core performance.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine with dedicated Intel Xeon cores and substantial local storage for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 physical cores at 2.0 GHz
448.0 GB of system memory providing 14.0 GB per core
Average multi-core and database benchmark performance
Weak single-core processing speeds representing a compute tradeoff
Suited for large databases, caching layers, and memory-heavy workloads
